display.purpose: The single hero figure on a screen — budget remaining, donut percentage. At most one per screen.

body.purpose: List items, descriptions, form labels, button text.

label.purpose: Captions, badges, nav labels, store/category tags, timestamps.

shadows.name: hero-lift
shadows.purpose: The budget hero card and other primary headline surfaces. Rare — the system's strongest visual statement.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Standard content cards (promo rows, list items, stat tiles) — gentle ambient separation from Paper.

shadows.name: toast-lift
shadows.purpose: In-context confirmations and toasts — shadow tinted toward Terracotta to echo the accent.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Strong ease-out for entering elements and press feedback (--ease-out).

motion.name: ease-in-out
motion.purpose: On-screen movement, e.g. the budget ring's stroke-dashoffset animation (--ease-in-out).

motion.name: press-feedback
motion.value: scale(0.97) over 60ms on :active, 120ms ease-out on release
motion.purpose: The .pressable pattern applied to every tappable surface in the system.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled terracotta call-to-action button with press feedback.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #e8712a; color: #ffffff; font-weight: 600; font-size: 14px; padding: 10px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 12px; transition: background-color 200ms ease, transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #cf5811; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(232,113,42,0.4); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); transition-duration: 60ms; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #7a7a8e; font-weight: 500; font-size: 13px; border: none; padding: 8px 4px; transition: transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(232,113,42,0.4); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: scale(0.97); transition-duration: 60ms; }

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Standard content card — number-and-label tile (history stats, dashboard quick actions).
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e8e8ee; border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(26,26,46,0.06); } .ds-card-label { margin: 0 0 2px; font-size: 12px; color: #7a7a8e; } .ds-card-value { margin: 0; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #e8712a; }

components.name: Hero Budget Card
components.description: The gradient hero surface showing remaining budget — the system's one Display-scale figure per screen.
components.html:
Оставащ бюджет

€157.20
components.css: .ds-hero { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #e8712a, #cf5811); color: #ffffff; border-radius: 20px; padding: 24px 22px 20px; box-shadow: 0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); } .ds-hero-label { margin: 0 0 4px; font-size: 13px; opacity: 0.85; } .ds-hero-value { margin: 0; font-size: 38px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: -0.02em; line-height: 1.1; } .ds-hero-track { height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); margin: 16px 0; overflow: hidden; } .ds-hero-bar { height: 100%; width: 62%; border-radius: 9999px; background: #ffffff; transition: width 1000ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Store/category filter pill — active state is the only colored element in its row.
components.css: .ds-chips { display: flex; gap: 8px; } .ds-chip { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid #e8e8ee; background: #ffffff; color: #7a7a8e; transition: background-color 200ms ease, color 200ms ease, transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-chip:active { transform: scale(0.97); transition-duration: 60ms; } .ds-chip-active { background: #e8712a; color: #ffffff; border-color: #e8712a; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field with terracotta focus halo.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; font-size: 14px; color: #1a1a2e; background: transparent; border: 1.5px solid #e8e8ee; border-radius: 12px; padding: 10px 14px; transition: border-color 150ms ease, box-shadow 150ms ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #7a7a8e; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #e8712a; box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(232,113,42,0.15); }

components.name: Bottom Tab Bar
components.description: Sticky 5-tab bottom navigation — the active tab is the only colored element, marked by a small floating accent bar.
components.html: НачалоПромоции
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; justify-content: space-around; align-items: stretch; background: #ffffff; border-top: 1px solid #e8e8ee; padding: 8px 4px 6px; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 2px; padding: 4px 8px; position: relative; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 120ms cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1); } .ds-nav-item:active { transform: scale(0.97); transition-duration: 60ms; } .ds-nav-label { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; color: #7a7a8e; } .ds-nav-label-active { font-weight: 700; color: #e8712a; } .ds-nav-indicator { position: absolute; top: -8px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); width: 20px; height: 3px; border-radius: 4px; background: #e8712a; }

narrative.overview:
Пазарувай умно ("Shop smart") is a personal grocery-budgeting companion: set a budget, track active promotions across seven Bulgarian retail chains, build a shopping list, and watch what you spent and saved settle into place. The system reads like a well-kept paper receipt redrawn for a screen — orderly rows, clear totals, generous quiet space, and exactly one warm color reserved for the things that matter: progress, savings, deals, and calls to action. Everything else stays calm and out of the way, because this is an app opened many times a day for a quick glance, not a destination to linger in.

It explicitly rejects the gray, joyless density of spreadsheet-style budgeting tools and the templated SaaS-dashboard look (hero-metric clichés, identical flat cards, gradient text, decorative glassmorphism). Nothing here should feel borrowed from an English-first finance template — the type, the numbers, the store names and the currency are native to the Bulgarian grocery market, and the design should feel made for it.
keyCharacteristics: One warm terracotta accent carries meaning (money, progress, savings, action); everything else is quiet ink-on-paper neutrals.
Numbers are the hero: large, extrabold, tabular-feeling figures for budget, spend and savings, never competing with decoration.
Calm by default, lifted at the moments that deserve attention — the budget hero card, completion toasts, freshly-added items.
Every surface, label and store name is written for a Bulgarian grocery shopper, not translated from a generic template.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Terracotta appears with intent — action, progress, or a win — never as decoration. If a screen has more than a small handful of terracotta touches, something is being marked that doesn't deserve marking.

rules.name: The Tonal Surface Rule
rules.body: Depth between Paper and Paper Card comes from a half-step lightness shift plus a hairline border, not from gray fills or shadows at rest. Shadows are reserved for elements that should visually float.

rules.name: The One Hero Number Rule
rules.body: Each screen gets exactly one Display-scale figure — the thing the user opened the app to check. Everything else steps down at least one full hierarchy level so that number reads instantly.

rules.name: The Earned Lift Rule
rules.body: A shadow is a claim that something deserves the user's attention right now. Reserve it for the hero figure, confirmations, and momentary feedback — not for every card on the screen.

dos: Do keep Terracotta (#e8712a) to the handful of elements that represent action, progress, or a win on any given screen — the One Accent Rule.
Do give the most important figure on each screen Display scale (38px/800) and let everything else step down — the One Hero Number Rule.
Do apply .pressable (scale(0.97) on :active, 120ms var(--ease-out)) to every tappable surface; this is opened many times a day and must always feel instantly responsive.

Do use the warm-tonal pairing (Paper #f5f5f7 → Paper Card #ffffff → Hairline #e8e8ee) to build depth before reaching for shadows; reserve Hero Lift and Card Lift shadows for surfaces that should visually float.
Do write every label, button, and empty-state in Bulgarian with EUR formatting via formatEUR() — this is a Bulgarian-market product, not a translated template.
donts: Don't build the gray, joyless spreadsheet look — no flat gray fills, no muted-on-muted text. Body and secondary text must clear 4.5:1 against Paper and Paper Card.
Don't reach for hero-metric clichés, identical flat card grids, or gradient text — the templated-SaaS-dashboard look this system explicitly rejects.
Don't use border-left/border-right colored stripes as accents on cards or list rows. Mark state with fill, the chip pattern, or the floating nav indicator instead.
Don't introduce a second saturated color outside the budget ring's >90% red warning — that is the one deliberate exception, not a precedent.

Don't animate beyond transform/opacity/stroke-dashoffset (and color, for state changes); this keeps frequent daily interactions fast and battery-friendly, and always pair motion with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
